2606.19965.title: ROSE: Benchmarking the Perception-to-Action Gap in Multimodal Models
2606.19965.paper_summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly expected to act on visual information, yet the same scene may require different actions under different task contexts. How reliably can a model turn the same visual evidence into the action required by the current context? To answer this question, we introduce \textsc{ROSE} (\textbf{R}eference-conditioned \textbf{O}ddity and \textbf{S}ymbolic \textbf{E}xecution), a controlled benchmark that holds the visual scene fixed while varying region constraints and required symbolic outputs. Through coupled counting and coordinate-action tasks, \textsc{ROSE} tests whether models can infer an implicit majority reference and act on the resulting fine-grained visual evidence under changing contexts. Across nine recent MLLMs, performance drops by as much as 44.5 percentage points from counting-oriented tasks to region-conditioned action, despite 98.8\% human performance. The gap persists on paired scenes and regions for which the same model returns the correct count, while global-click and matched local controls show that coordinate grounding explains only part of the loss, revealing a distinct, model-dependent bottleneck in turning shared visual evidence into context-specific actions.
2606.19965.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）越來越被期望能夠處理視覺信息，但同一場景在不同的任務背景下可能需要不同的行動。模型能多可靠地將相同的視覺證據轉化為當前背景所需的行動？為了回答這個問題，我們介紹了\textsc{ROSE}（\textbf{R}eference-conditioned \textbf{O}ddity和\textbf{S}ymbolic \textbf{E}xecution），這是一個控制基準，保持視覺場景不變，同時改變區域約束和所需的符號輸出。通過耦合計數和坐標行動任務，\textsc{ROSE}測試模型是否能推斷出隱含的主要參考，並在變化的背景下根據結果的細粒度視覺證據進行行動。在九個最近的MLLMs中，從以計數為導向的任務到區域條件行動的性能下降了多達44.5個百分點，儘管人類的表現達到98.8\%。在配對場景和區域中，當同一模型返回正確計數時，這一差距仍然存在，而全局點擊和匹配的局部控制顯示坐標基礎僅解釋了部分損失，揭示了將共享的視覺證據轉化為特定上下文行動的明顯、依賴模型的瓶頸。
2606.19965.author: Yihao Wang et.al.
2606.19965.authors: Yihao Wang, Zijian He, Jie Ren, Keze Wang
